{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],

  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The agent output does not provide sufficient evidence to support the claim that temperature scaling is the de facto current standard for confidence calibration in deep ASR systems.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty claim is overstated, as token- and word-level confidence in transformer/RNN-T ASR has been studied repeatedly.",
    "R1.7": "The agent output does not provide sufficient evidence to support the claim that the proposed method achieves better calibration than existing methods.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty disclosure: The agent output does not clearly indicate the uncertainty associated with each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence: The agent output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it focuses on ASR systems rather than tool-use agents.",
    "RX.4": "Downstream-harm avoidance: The agent output introduces new errors and fabrications by misattributing the contribution of Hannun et al. (2014) and overstating the novelty of the proposed method.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability: The agent output does not provide sufficient information to track the sources and changes made in the proposed method."
  },

    "RX.1": 0.9,

    "RX.5": 0.3
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The agent output does not provide sufficient evidence to support the claim that temperature scaling is the de facto current standard for confidence calibration in deep ASR systems.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim is overstated, as token- and word-level confidence in transformer/RNN-T ASR has been studied repeatedly.
rationale.R1.7: The agent output does not provide sufficient evidence to support the claim that the proposed method achieves better calibration than existing methods.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure: The agent output does not clearly indicate the uncertainty associated with each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence: The agent output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it focuses on ASR systems rather than tool-use agents.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance: The agent output introduces new errors and fabrications by misattributing the contribution of Hannun et al. (2014) and overstating the novelty of the proposed method.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability: The agent output does not provide sufficient information to track the sources and changes made in the proposed method.
